item.title: www.arafathasan.live-এ অংশগ্রহণের নীতিমালা
item.description: www.arafathasan.live-এ আপনাকে স্বাগত। এখানে আপনার অংশগ্রহণে আমরা অত্যন্ত আনন্দিত। আমরা আশা করি যে প্রত্যেক অংশগ্রহণকারী এই জায়গাকে তার নিজের বাড়ির মতো মনে করবে এবং নিশ্চয়তা দিতে চাই যাতে সকলে অন্য অংশগ্রহণকারীদের নিজের বাড়ির সদস্য মনে করে তদ্রুপ আচরণ করে। এ উদ্দেশ্যে আমাদের কিছু দায়িত্ব পালন করতে হয়, বাড়ির উপযুক্ত পরিবেশ নিশ্চিতে মন্তব্য মুছে দেবার মতো কঠিন কাজও করতে হয়। এ জন্য আমাদের একটি নীতিমালাও প্রণয়ন করতে হয়েছে। আমরা জানি যে এই নীতিমালার সবই আপনি জানেন, বোঝেন এবং মানেন, তারপরেও আমরা আপনাকে নীতিমালাটি পড়ে দেখতে অনুরোধ করছি।

item.title: LAMP Installation and Config
item.description: Install phpMyAdmin on Centos 8 Step 1: Install phpMyAdmin The phpMyAdmin tool is not included in the default CentOS 8 repositories. However, the file can be download manually. wget https://files.phpmyadmin.net/phpMyAdmin/4.9.4/phpMyAdmin-4.9.4-all-languages.zip Extract the .zip file archive: unzip phpMyAdmin-4.9.4-all-languages.zip Move the extracted files to the /usr/share/phpmyadmin directory: sudo mv phpMyAdmin-4.9.4-all-languages.zip /usr/share/phpmyadmin Change directories: cd /usr/share/phpmyadmin Rename the sample php configuration file: sudo mv config.

item.title: Redis Notes
item.description: 1. Introduction to Redis Redis is an acronym for Remote dictionary server. It’s an open source, in-memory and persistent key-value database / store which stores data as key-value pairs and also doubles up as a message broker. Redis supports a wide array of data structures including sets, lists, hashes, strings, HyperLogLogs and so many more. What is a key-value pair? A key-value pair is a set or pair of two linked items.

item.title: PostgresSQL Notes
item.description: Table of Content Introduction to PostgreSQL 🚧 Installation Install PostgreSQL in Ubuntu Install PostgreSQL in CentOS Method 1: PostgreSQL Yum Repository Method 2: Using DNF Initial Configuration Creating a New PostgreSQL Database Cluster Understanding PostgreSQL Roles and Databases Creating a New Postgres Role Some Very First Commands Examples CREATE DATABASE DROP DATABASE CREATE TABLE Table Definition INSERT INTO SELECT DROP TABLE The Surface Sea Mockaroo Data Generator ORDER BY ASC DESC ORDER BY with Two-parameter DISTINCT WHERE BETWEEN LIKE GROUP BY GROUP BY with ORDER BY GROUP BY HAVING COALESCE Another Table Called car Basic Functions MAX MIN AVG ROUND COUNT SUM Basic Arithmetic Operations Discount Calculation ALIAS NULLIF DATE NOW Addition and Subtraction of Date INTERVAL EXTRACT AGE The Shallow Sea PRIMARY KEY CONSTRAINTS UNIQUE constraint CHECK Constraint DELETE UPDATE ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING DO UPDATE SET Foreign Keys, Joins and Relationships Delete Record with Foreign Keys JOIN INNER JOIN LEFT JOIN RIGHT JOIN FULL OUTER JOIN Exporting Query Results to CSV Serials and Sequences Extensions UUID Datatype UUID as Primary Key Introduction to PostgreSQL PostgreSQL is a powerful, open source object-relational database system with over 30 years of active development that has earned it a strong reputation for reliability, feature robustness, and performance.

item.title: About Me
item.description: Last semester student of Computer Science and Engineering at Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University. Super computer nerd who loves Vim, Linux and enjoys to customize all of the development environment. Work best with keyboard shortcuts and hate to use a mouse. Interested in devising a better problem-solving method for challenging tasks, the harder it is the more thrilled I feel. Inquisitive of learning new technologies and tools if the need arises.
